title: For Christmas Eve
scripture_text: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased.
reflection: The gospel begins not with a king, but with a baby, in a feed trough, in a barn. God chose the bottom of the pile to enter the world.
body:
The candles are lit. The songs are being sung. The children are asleep on the floor, dreaming of the morning.

Tonight, the world tilted. A long time ago, in a place you have never been, a woman gave birth, and the world broke open.

He was not what anyone expected. He came quietly, to the wrong family, in the wrong town, at the wrong time of year. He came to the bottom. He came to the people no one was looking at.

And that is good news, tonight, for you. The God who runs the universe has been, for a long time, on the side of the overlooked. Of the small. Of the tired. Of you.

Sleep well. He is near.
prayer: Lord, You came. Thank You for coming. Thank You for coming as a baby, so we would not be afraid. Amen.
